sections.paragraph:
m. with suff. שִׁלְחֹו-

(1) a weapon, missile, as sent against an enemy; Arab. سِلَحُ and سِلاَحُ coll. arms; specially a sword; سَالِحُ armed; سَلَحَ Conj. V., to arm oneself, 2Ch 32:5 23:10 Joe 2:8. עָבַר בַּשֶּׁלַח to perish by the weapon (of death), Job 33:18 36:12 .

(2) a shoot, a sprout, Son 4:13 compare the root Piel No. 4.

(3) [Selah, Shelah], pr. name-(a) of a son of Arphaxad, Gen 10:24 11:12 .-(b) of an aqueduct and pool near Jerusalem, which appears to be the same as שִׁלֹחַ (which see) Neh 3:15. Vulg. Siloe.
